title: Management consultants and business analysts
description: Analyze and coordinate the ongoing logistical functions of a firm or organization. Responsible for the entire life cycle of a product, including acquisition, distribution, internal allocation, delivery, and final disposal of resources. Design or analyze operational solutions for projects such as transportation optimization, network modeling, process and methods analysis, cost containment, capacity enhancement, routing and shipment optimization, or information management. Analyze product delivery or supply chain processes to identify or recommend changes. May manage route activity including invoicing, electronic bills, and shipment tracing. (Based on 14 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Conduct research to gather information about survey topics and consult with clients to identify specific survey needs or requirements.
Analyze data from surveys, old records, or case studies, and specify manipulative or computational methods to be applied to models using statistical software.
Prepare management reports defining and evaluating problems, recommending solutions, and presenting summaries and analyses of survey data.
Develop business methods and procedures, including accounting systems, office systems, logistics systems, and production schedules.
Analyze information obtained from management to conceptualize and define operational problems, and study alternative courses of action for the best outcomes.
Collaborate with others in the organization to ensure successful implementation of chosen solutions and to achieve management objectives.
Develop mathematical or statistical models of phenomena, and evaluate statistical methods used to obtain and analyze data.
Conduct surveys and collect data through methods such as interviews, questionnaires, focus groups, and market analysis surveys.
Analyze information to identify significant relationships and trends in data while preparing to report results in various formats.
Collaborate with researchers in the planning, implementation, and evaluation of surveys, and provide biostatistical consultation to clients or colleagues.
Develop and maintain client relationships while understanding customers' needs to ensure those needs are met.
Identify cost-reduction or process-improvement logistic opportunities, and evaluate the effectiveness of current or future logistical processes.
Analyze logistics data, using methods such as data mining or cost-benefit analysis, and prepare reports on logistics performance measures.
Conduct logistics studies or analyses, such as time studies or flow-path analyses, and develop or maintain cost estimates or forecasts.
Manage the logistical aspects of product life cycles and provide logistical facility or capacity planning analyses for distribution or transportation functions.
Support the development and implementation of training materials and technical manuals, and prepare or validate documentation for logistics processes.
Develop logistic metrics, internal analysis tools, or key performance indicators for business units to improve efficiency.
Collaborate with other departments and confer with logistics management teams to optimize service levels and minimize costs.
Analyze data gathered on problems or procedures, and document findings to prepare recommendations for new systems or organizational changes.
Design comprehensive supply chains that minimize environmental impacts or costs, and analyze environmental impact or energy efficiency of logistics activities.
Review forms and reports, and confer with management and users about format, distribution, and purpose, identifying problems and improvements.
